集训3 · 假期复习任务卡 · Day 6 of 24

The Bird in the Yard

📖 Reading

328 words · 28 target words · listen while you read

Tier 1（8词） Tier 2（6词） Tier 3（14词）

Zoe was new at school, and every recess felt terrible. She sat alone near the library, feeling bored and forgetful of how to make friends.

One day, a boy named Sam came over. He was friendly and had an attractive smile that made Zoe feel welcome right away. "Hi, I'm Sam," he said, and he began to talk about his favorite hobby: watching birds.

"Come with me after school," Sam said. "There's a bird with a hurt wing down at the beach. I want to check on it." Zoe felt a little scared at first — she was quite short and did not want to climb over slippery rocks or lose her footing on wet stones. But Sam's kindness made it easy to decide to go.

When they got there, a small wave rolled up over the sand and then pulled back. They found the bird sitting still nearby. Sam gave a soft laugh and said the bird just needed rest, not a rescue. Zoe felt embarrassed for worrying so much, but Sam did not tease her at all.

They watched the bird for a while, listening to seagulls and soft music drifting from a radio down the shore. Sam explained the order of things he had learned in his last bird-watching lesson: watch quietly, keep your distance, and never touch a wild animal. Zoe thought it was the opposite of boring — it was the most interesting afternoon she'd had in weeks, and she felt her confidence slowly rise with every minute.

The next morning, Zoe wanted to send Sam a thank-you letter, so she wrote one at home and asked her mom to receive it and mail it that day. She did not want to introduce herself to anyone else that week; she had already found a real friend.

By Friday, Zoe would regret nothing about her first difficult weeks at the new school, because Sam had turned everything around, one small kindness at a time.

🔍 Reading Comprehension

5 questions · tap to check your answer

1In "She sat alone near the library, feeling bored and forgetful of how to make friends," what does bored mean?
B — Zoe had nothing interesting to do at recess, which is what feeling bored means.
2In "He was friendly and had an attractive smile that made Zoe feel welcome right away," what does friendly mean?
A — Sam's warmth made Zoe feel welcome, showing he was kind and pleasant.
3In "Zoe felt embarrassed for worrying so much, but Sam did not tease her at all," what does embarrassed mean?
B — Zoe felt a little ashamed that she had worried over nothing, but Sam was kind about it.
4Why did Zoe feel scared about going to the beach with Sam?
C — The story says she was short and did not want to climb over slippery rocks or lose her footing on wet stones.
5How did Zoe feel about her first weeks at the new school by the end of the story?
B — The story ends by saying Zoe would regret nothing about her difficult first weeks, since Sam had turned things around.
🔁
